observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

1.	Direct the Inventory Committee to:

a.	Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan and conduct the physical count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) in accordance with the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to ensure the proper implementation of the one-time cleansing and reconciliation of PPE account balances; 



b.	Ensure the timely submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) to the Audit Team by January 31 each year, and reconcile the results of the report with the accounting records.





2.	Require the Municipal Accountant to:

a.	Derecognize from the PPE accounts the items costing less than P50,000.00 and apply the guidelines stated in COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024 regarding their reclassification to semi-expendable property, inventory, or expense, as applicable.  Henceforth, apply the P50,000.00 capitalization threshold when recording PPE;




b.	Exert earnest effort in the preparation and maintenance of PPE Ledger Cards, ensuring that all records are accurate, complete, and reconciled with the property cards and RPCPPE.





c.	Require the Property Custodian to assist the Municipal Accountant in the proper reclassification of items costing less than P50,000.00 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024, ensuring that these are correctly transferred to the appropriate semi-expendable property, inventory, or expense accounts.  Additionally, facilitate the replacement of Property Cards with Semi-Expendable Property Cards, where applicable, ensuring that all records accurately reflect the classification, accountability, and movement of assets. |

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Instruct the concerned departments or offices responsible for recording the municipal's road network to collaborate and develop a systematic approach to meet the accounting and reporting requirements stipulated in COA Circular No. 2015-008; and
Reach out to the DILG Regional Office VII to inquire about training schedules for the LRAMS. If such training is available, the concerned officials are encouraged to attend.

We recommended and the Municipal GSO agreed to exert efforts in identifying the composition and specification of the said lumped balance or expedite the LGU's compliance with the One-time Cleansing of PPE account balances prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the Management implement the following courses of actions:
The Municipal Mayor issue a supplementary EO to include in the duties and functions of the existing Inventory Committee the actual physical count of supplies or create a separate Inventory Committee for the said purpose, whichever is practicable;
The Municipal Mayor totally relieve the Municipal GSO from doing accounting functions to be able to focus on the mandated functions of the office as provided for in RA No. 7160; and
The Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant-Designate maintain their respective required records pertaining to supplies.

We recommended that the Municipal GSO work closely with the Municipal Engineer in retrieving the plans and drawings of all the buildings and structures owned by the LGU. If these documents cannot be located, we suggested that the GSO inquire with the GSIS regarding any alternative documents that may serve in lieu of the plans and drawings, otherwise, the Municipal Engineer should prepare such necessary documents.

We recommended the following courses of actions:
Management, through the Municipal Accountant, ensure that cash advances are liquidated by issuing demand letters to the AOs and refrain from granting additional cash advances to the same officials or employees who have yet to liquidate their previous cash advances pursuant to Sec. 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002. If the AOs do not pay attention to the demand letters, consider taking legal actions against them; and
For the unliquidated ₱364,600.08 cash advance of a deceased AO and whose request for write-off had been denied, Management take the necessary legal action to recover said amount.

We recommended that given the urgent nature of the financial aid, the MSWDO streamline its existing procedures to shorten the processing time from the assessment date to the actual payout date so as not to defeat the purpose of the fund/ program.
We likewise recommended that the MSWDO revisit the actual expenditures over the past years and make logical estimates to be proposed in the succeeding budget calls, otherwise, revisit the approved matrix of financial assistance for possible adjustment of rates/amount based on the approved budget for the year.

We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Human Resource Office, reevaluate its PRAISE System and implement necessary revisions including, specific and clear criteria for each award category, the documentary requirements, the nomination procedures, timelines, and the deliberation process by the PRAISE Committee. Thereafter, secure approval from the Civil Service Commission as required under Item No. 6 of CSC MC No. 1 series of 2001. Pending CSC approval, we recommended that Management refrain from further giving the same incentive as this may fall under Irregular Expenditure under COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended and Management agreed to implement the following courses of action:
Prepare and submit to the Audit Team at the beginning of the year, the list of all on-going Government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year and should be updated every quarter following the format prescribed by COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
Inform the SA and the ATL within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

We recommended and Management, through the Chairperson of the GFPS, agreed to develop a structured monitoring system on the status of implementation of different GAD programs and activities embodied in the GPB, and ensure to implement them based on the target implementation timeframe.
We also recommended and the MSWDO agreed to ensure to review the proposed GAD programs and activities to avoid redundancy or duplication of similar programs, so as to allow the use of funds to some activities and programs that effectively address gender-related issues.

We recommended and Management, through the LDRRM Council, agreed to present distinctively the allocation intended for the 30 per cent QRF in lump-sum, and consider allocating the remaining 70 per cent that encompass the required four thematic areas, to ensure that funds are readily available at every stage of a calamity or emergency, from prevention and mitigation to preparedness, response, and recovery.

We recommended and the Management, through the LDRRM Officer, agreed to submit a written explanation or justification together with documentations supporting the validity of the above transactions charged against the QRF.
We also recommended to which the Municipal Budget and the LDRRM Officers agreed to ensure that the 30 per cent QRF be used only during a state of calamity duly declared by the appropriate local sanggunian or a Presidential declaration upon recommendation of the NDRRMC pursuant to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to issue a Memorandum/Order to enforce the P5,000.00 net take home policy among the employees of the LGU in compliance with Section 56 of the General Appropriations Act of CY 2025. 

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:

a.	Human Resource and Management Officer and the Payroll Maker to stop the practice of certifying loan application of employees whose net take home pay falls below the minimum take home pay of P5,000.00; 



b.	Payroll Maker/Municipal Accountant to issue Certificate of Net Take Home Pay as a requirement prior to the certification/confirmation/approval of loan application; and



c.	Municipal Accountant to stop remitting loan amortizations for the payment of personal loans which were not deducted from the payroll. 

This is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor will issue a Memorandum Order enforcing the employees to adhere the P5,000.00 net take home policy among the employees of the LGU in compliance to existing guidelines.



Loan Applicants are required to submit Certification of Net Take Home Pay from the Mun. Accountant to ensure that their net take home pay will not fall below the minimum which is P5,000.00. 

The Municipal Accountant will issue Certificate of Net Take Home Pay as requirement prior to the approval of a certain loan, ensuring that they did not fall below the minimum net take home pay.

The Municipal Accountant will commit to stop remitting loan amortizations for the payment of personal loans which are not deducted from the payroll.
observations.reason:
For Implementation




The Private Secretary of the Municipal Mayor has already been informed for the issuance of the said Memorandum Order

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor, through the Human Resource Management Officer, to:

a.	Provide justification on the approval to send the job order personnel to attend meetings/trainings and to undertake travels amounting to P39,962.00, as presented in Table 1 despite the absence of duties and responsibilities in the contract of service; and

b.	Henceforth, provide and submit certification that the trainings attended and the travels undertaken by the job order personnel are necessary in the fulfillment of their obligations duly approved by the Head of Agency and/or any authorized official and a certification that the travel could not be performed by regular personnel. 


We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to process training and travel related-transactions of the job order personnel provided these are duly supported with certification that the related travels and/or trainings were necessary in the fulfillment of their obligations and a certification that the travel could not be performed by regular personnel.
observations.managementAction:
The Heads of Office are required to provide justifications for approving their staff who are job order workers to send to meetings/trainings 

In addition to the justification, the Head of Office is required to certify that the trainings to be attended by their job order personnel are necessary in the fulfillment of their obligations and that the said travel could not be performed by other regular personnel.
The Municipal Accountant will ensure that in processing trainings and travel related transactions of the job order personnel, these are duly supported with certifications required.

observations.recommendation:

c.	Maximize the utilization of GAD funds to deliver benefits for gender equality and women empowerment.

To ensure that GAD issues are clearly stated and corresponding PPAs are aligned with GAD mandates, further citing the necessary provisions of the laws and Sustainable Development Goals that support such initiative.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned in ensuring the full implementation of the five priority PPAs in CY 2024 under the 20%DF as embodied in the AIP and the optimal utilization of the funds in compliance with the provisions of Item 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020. We recommend the Management to continually make a conscious effort to accomplish the identified development projects efficiently and effectively in accordance with rules and regulations.

Further, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to require Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to, ensure that disbursements of the projects funded by the 20% Development Fund are within their corresponding appropriation, otherwise, supplemental budget should be prepared to support the excess disbursements. Henceforth, also implement measures on the monitoring of the utilization of the fund to ensure that disbursements are within their corresponding appropriation.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality of Liloan will commit to do the best efforts that in the year 2025 and onwards, the full implementation of the PPAs under the 20% DF will be maintained in compliance to its provisions on optimizing the utilization of the funds.


The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer will be cautious and will make an implementing measures to ensure that disbursement of projects funded by the 20% DF are within the corresponding appropriation and any excess thereof will also be supported with supplemental budget.
observations.reason: Physical implementation of the projects is still going on. No disbursements has been made for the current appropriated projects except for the payment of loans.

observations.recommendation:

We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local Peace and Order Council include only those PPAs that are directly related to crime prevention and law enforcement activities, capability development for personnel of law enforcement agencies, volunteers/partners, or programs for anti illegal drug, illegal gambling, counter-insurgency, and/or counter-terrorism efforts, among others that will directly contribute to the maintenance of the peace and order and public safety and henceforth, for Confidential Funds: 

c.	Strictly comply with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG- Governance Commission for GOCCs-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the entitlement, release, use and reporting of Confidential Funds.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor will direct the Municipal Budget Officer to rigorously adhere to the guidelines outlined in Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-02 concerning the policies and procedures in the proper appropriation of both the Peace and Order Program and Confidential Funds and that to include only in the appropriation of the Peace and Order and Public Safety those PPAs that are directly related to the fund.

On the other hand, whenever expenditures as appropriate to be charged to the Confidential Fund, the supporting documentary evidence of the payment, among others, be submitted to ICFAU in signed envelope signed by the Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) with Certification of such Accountable Officer of the Confidential Funds signed under oath.

observations.recommendation:
1. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
1. Instruct the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Property
Division/Unit and the Accounting Unit, to implement the One-Time
Cleansing of PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006;
2. Require the Supply and Property Officer-Designate to prepare the
Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties for all PPEs
found unserviceable; and
3. Require the Municipal Accountant to exert earnest effort in the
preparation and maintenance of PPE ledger cards and reconcile with the
property cards and RPCPPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly require the Revenue
Collection Clerks and Liquidating Officer to remit their collections intact to
the Municipal Treasurer and the latter to deposit intact to the Authorized
Government Depository Bank all his/her collections daily or not later than
the next banking day. Further, require the RCCs, Liquidating Officer and
the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the prepared Report of Collections
and Deposit are duly dated and signed. This is without prejudice of the
issuance of Notice of Charge as a result of our Cash Examination.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Health
Officer, to:
1. Secure Certification by the Secretary of the DOST/DOH/DND/Director of
the PHILVOCS that the place of assignment/travel is a strife-
torn/embattled/disease-infested/distress or isolated areas/stations, or
areas declared under state of calamity or emergency, or with volcano
activity or eruption; and
2. Attach the complete supporting documents to the payrolls/vouchers.

We likewise recommended the Municipal Accountant to process
disbursement vouchers only when they are supported with complete
documentation, and adhere strictly to the guidelines on the grant of hazard
pay, subsistence, and laundry allowance.

We recommended the Public Health Workers presented in Table 7 to refund
the amount equivalent to the overpaid benefits.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Property Custodian
to:
a. Use the required format of a sequentially numbered Driver’s Trip Ticket
duly approved by the Agency Head or the authorized representative with
complete information being filled out in every detail needed for all travels
undertaken;
b. Reconcile every month the Driver’s Trip Ticket with the Monthly Fuel
Consumption Report and Summary of Monthly Report of Official
Travels as mandated under COA Circular No. 77-61 to determine
whether the said fuel usage is valid and is being used appropriately;
c. Utilize the prescribed form such as the Requisition and Issue Voucher,
Summary of Requisition and Issue Voucher, Daily Gas Issue Record,
Gasoline Control Card as a tool in the monitoring of the receipt and
issuance of the fuel;
d. Enforce the “No Properly filled-up Driver’s Trip Ticket, No Trip Policy”;
e. As to the distance travelled, to determine the accurate consumption of
fuel:
- All vehicles with functional odometer should record the kilometer
reading before and after the trip
- All vehicles with non-functional/defective odometer should indicate
the approximate distance traveled including the number of trips
undertaken during the day. Immediate repair of the odometer should
be considered.
f. Submit to the Audit Team the reconciliation of the differences in the
number of liters of fuel between the Consumption Report and the
Withdrawal Slip aggregating to 655 liters and account for the
discrepancies between the number of liters procured in CY 2023 versus
the number of liters utilized as reported in the Fuel Consumption Report
vis-a-vis the Withdrawal Slips aggregating to 5,196 liters and 4,621
liters, respectively. Once accounted, submit to the Audit Team the list of
vehicles which consumed the said number of liters of fuel.

observations.recommendation:
1.29 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:
1. Municipal Accountant and Supply and Property Officer to reconcile their
records with the RPCPPE and adjust the books of accounts and the
records of Supply and Property Officer accordingly, to take up the
missing and/or unrecorded/unlocated PPEs;
2. Supply and Property Officer, in coordination with the Municipal
Accountant to establish accountability over the PPE not accounted for in
the physical count and take appropriate action to recover the
replacement value of the said equipment from the accountable officer
concerned, if any;
3. Supply and Property Officer to update the IIRUP prepared, if needed, as
basis of the Municipal Accountant to derecognize from the books the cost
of unserviceable PPE and the corresponding accumulated depreciation;
and
4. The Supply and Property Officer to use the prescribed property number
format under COA Circular No. 2020-006 in order to have a uniform
property identification system.

2.16 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:
1. Municipal Accountant to:
a. Exert earnest effort to obtain supporting documents from the bank to
further analyze the unidentified reconciling items in the General Fund
and Special Education Fund totaling P109,628.38 and record the same
in the books to accurately present the cash account balance, if
warranted;
b. Reconcile the difference between the balances of the General Ledger
and the Statement of Financial Position of the Philhealth Capitation
Fund amounting to P1,500.00; and
c. Record the reconciling items relative to the bank charges totaling
P9,200.00 incurred by the Municipality due to the failure of the
Municipal Treasurer to act appropriately as to insufficient balance of
the bank account. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice
of Disallowance as a result of our audit.
2. Municipal Treasurer to make representation with the authorized
depository bank to confirm the existence of the Cash in Bank, Local
Currency Time deposit account totaling P88,521.00.

3.7 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Accountant and/or Department Heads to:
a. Exert earnest effort to retrieve the supporting documents relative to the
fund transfers amounting to P1,380,119.47;
b. Effect the proper liquidation of all fund transfers after the completion of
the project as required in COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13,
1994; and
c. Send confirmation letters to the Source Agencies indicating therein the
name of the project and the corresponding unliquidated balance, if any.

4.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer and Collecting Officers that:
a. The official receipts be written in hard indelible ballpen and the duplicate
and triplicate copies will be clear carbon reproductions in all respects of
whatever may have been written on the original;
b. Carbon paper must not be over-used to the extent that what is written on
the duplicate and triplicate cannot be read and recognized;
c. Duplicate copies to be submitted to the Audit Team should be carbon
reproductions of the original and not photocopies of the triplicate; and
d. Henceforth, prioritize the safekeeping of the official receipts as required in
Section 101 of PD 1445.

5.15 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Require all the Revenue Collection Clerks to remit to the Liquidating
Officer/ Municipal Treasurer the full amount of their collections on a
daily basis as required by COA Circular No. 92-382; and
b. Direct the Liquidating Officer and/or Municipal Treasurer to indicate the
date of receipt of collections and remittances from the RCCs and ensure
that the deposit of the remittances to the Authorized Government
Depository Banks are made daily or on the next banking day.

6.22 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC and BAC
Secretariat to:
a. Submit justification why the five procurement contracts were awarded
despite that these were not duly supported with complete eligibility
requirements namely the Statement of On-going Contracts, Statement of
Single and Largest Completed Contract, NFCC and posting
requirements of the Supplemental Bid Bulletin, Notice of Award, Notice
to Proceed and Contract Agreement, among others;
b. Submit to the Audit Team the Disbursement Vouchers and its necessary
documents supporting the procurement of two infrastructure contracts
amounting to P6,145,780.13 for proper disposition in audit; and
c. Evaluate carefully the eligibility requirements submitted by the interested
bidders so that any lacking documents or misrepresentations will be
detected and to declare as eligible only those who comply with the
provisions of the RA 9184 and its 2016 RIRR.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance as
a result of our audit.

7.18 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Ensure the implementation of all approved PPAs for the optimal
utilization of the appropriation for development projects and warrant
that the projects to be funded out of the 20% are well planned and
procurement and implementation ready; and
b. Direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and
Development Officer to fast tract the implementation of the development
projects and exert earnest effort to regularly monitor the same as
required in DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-01
dated November 4, 2020 and Section 109 of the Local Government Code.

8.17 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the:
a. Human Resource Department to adopt the 2022 Guideline and
Standards in the design of the Municipality’s Organizational Staffing
and Structure Pattern and issue policies/guidelines/standards/procedures
relative to the hiring/retention/renewal of job order and casual
personnel; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all payments made to the job
order/casual personnel are duly acknowledged by the payees as required
in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

9.8 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer
to develop collection strategies and intensify the collection of real property
taxes and special education taxes to provide additional sources of funds of
the Municipality.

10.18 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Public Health Nurse
and the Municipal Health Officer as Vaccine Coordinators to:
1. Submit justification on the discrepancies among the Inventory Report,
Requisition and Issue Slip prepared by the Municipality vis-à-vis the
Inventory Report of the Province’s NIP Coordinator; and
2. Submit to the Audit Team the lacking necessary requirements relative to
the receipt and distribution of the covid vaccines and ancillary supplies
which includes the following: inspection and acceptance reports,
distribution reports, master list of vaccinated individuals, among others.

11.12 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Council ensure that the municipality is
prepared for calamities as prescribed in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 by procuring standard disaster response
equipment required in DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Concurrent Municipal Treasurer fully remit the ₱1,212,611.56 to the account of the DPWH through the Bureau of the Treasury (BTr), and immediately notify the latter through the National Building Code Development Office that its five per cent share has already been remitted.
We recommended that the Concurrent Municipal Treasurer refrain from collecting fire code fees and remit the ₱207,938.44 collections to the account of the Bureau of Fire Protection, through the BTr.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the prizes from Due to LGUs to Prior Period Adjustment (Miscellaneous Income) and coordinate with the Concurrent Municipal Treasurer to include these as a new funding source subject to budgetary laws and regulations.

The Concurrent Municipal Treasurer fully release the RPT shares due to the Province and the Barangays totaling ₱3,969,914.37.
The Municipal Accountant fast track the computation of the distribution of the RPT shares of the Barangays and immediately notify the Concurrent Municipal Treasurer for the release of such shares.
The Municipal Accountant monitor, through the subsidiary ledgers, the movement of the RPT shares and immediately notify the Concurrent Municipal Treasurer whenever there are discrepancies between the withheld and released RPT shares.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist, who is responsible in providing inventory of breeding stocks and its prevailing market price, fast track the conduct of the physical inventory of the Municipality’s breeding stocks taking into consideration its biological transformation and submit a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant to update its records and correct its balance in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, pending disposal, recognize additional impairment loss using the estimated recoverable service amount of each unserviceable property; and identify the components of the properties booked as “Other Assets/Unserviceable”, determine its useful life and compute the related depreciation per item.

We recommend that Management exert efforts to locate, produce and acquire documents evidencing acquisition and ownership of these lands; and record these lands in the books of the Municipality once the documents of acquisition and ownership are obtained.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to check for available funds in the General Fund-Proper that can accommodate the charging of these disbursements. We also recommended that henceforth, Management clearly specify the PPAs included in the LDRRM Fund Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries. We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer maintain a Registry of Appropriations, Allotments, and Obligations for the continuing appropriation of the LDRRM Fund for Capital Outlay.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exhaust all possible remedies to collect and to demand liquidation of cash advances especially from those who were already separated from the service, otherwise, request authority to write-off from COA, if warranted.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to demand from the accountable officers the return of all unserviceable properties and the Municipal General Services Officer to initiate the disposal of the listed unserviceable PPEs.
We recommended and Management agreed to secure and prepare all the legal documents necessary for land titling including those cited in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Concurrent Municipal Treasurer to locate the MOAs to these funds, review the terms and conditions therein and take appropriate action based on the said review; and to return the unutilized PDAF and CDF to the BTr.  We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant secure copies of the MOAs on fund transfers/assistance from the National Government and establish a system of control for the proper accounting and monitoring of the receipts and utilizations thereof, for easy reference.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Concurrent Municipal Treasurer for the return of the excess funds from completed projects.  We also recommended that for financial assistance which remained unutilized, Management secure authority from the Source Agency to transfer the same to the General Fund to be used for other priority projects.
We recommended that Management discontinue charging these expenditures under the 20 per cent Development Fund and instead provide appropriation for these under the General Fund-Proper.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer conduct judicious planning and scheduling to ensure implementation of its disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation PPAs.  We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor convene the LDRRM Council to review the current LDRRM Fund Investment Plan and assess whether the PPAs are implementable within the current year, and submit, if warranted, a revised or supplemental LDRRM Fund Investment Plan clearly specifying the PPAs therein to the Sanggunian for approval.

We recommended that Management endeavor to improve the procurement time on the approval of resolution/issuance of Notice of Award, and the contract preparation and signing so as to observe the required procurement timeline as provided in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and to achieve efficiency in the delivery of services to the requesting offices and the constituents.
We recommended that the BAC henceforth, strictly follow the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 specifically on the conduct of detailed evaluation of all bids.
We recommended that Management stop the practice of allowing contractors to start the projects even before the execution of the contract and the issuance of the NTPs.

We recommended that Management impose the recovery of appropriate liquidated damages from the supplier/contractor, otherwise hold the official/s responsible for the non-computation of such liquidated damages liable.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, after the defects liability period, which is one-year from the date of project completion, submit to the Municipal Mayor an Acceptance Report and the Warranty Certificate from the contractor that will serve as the basis of the latter in the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance.

Municipal Mayor must:

a. Require the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an
Ordinance for the augmentation/realignment of funds and a
supplemental budget for any changes in the Annual Budget to
cover new PPAs from realigned funds or available funds from new
revenue source as certified by the Municipal Treasurer.

Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to
prepare a supplemental AIP to support the changes in the Annual
budget.

We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive:

1. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct regular physical
inventory taking on all properties to validate all recorded balances
for PPE items. To facilitate said inventory count, each department
may conduct physical inventory on all properties within its office, to
be submitted for consolidation with those prepared by the others.

2. List all Property, Plant and Equipment found on hand, classifying
them into serviceable or unserviceable;

3. Maintain updated property cards per category on all property plant
and equipment as recorded in the list of Property, Plant and
Equipment of accounting records.

4. Reconcile property records ensuring that the balances shown in the
prepared RCPPE agree with the supply records and the book
balances in the accounting records, and vice versa;

5. Renew and maintain all acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment
(ARE);

6. Prepare the necessary Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property and Waste Materials Report for the
unserviceable properties found on hand, taking into consideration EO
No. 888 March 1983, COA Memorandum No. 2005-027;

7. Direct the Municipal Assessor to produce a listing of all the
properties owned by the government and reconcile them with those
properties counted by the committee;

8. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
determine the validity of the PPE account through reconciliation of
the accounting records and the actual inventory;

9. Require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Prepare the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC)
for each class of property, plant and equipment listing therein the
data as to its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of
depreciation, disposal, and other information about the property,
plant and equipment, based on the source documents of the
transactions;

b. Drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties
after the Waste Materials Report and the Inventory and Inspection
Report of Unserviceable Property are duly approved and evaluated
by the COA.

3.5 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to exert extra effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks
and require the Municipal Treasurer to follow the procedures stated on
Section 59 of NGAS for LGUs for spoiled and stale checks, as follows:
1. For spoiled checks which are immediately cancelled and for
which the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) has not yet been
prepared, the cancelled check shall be attached to the RCI
and reported chronologically with the other checks issued
and the word “Cancelled” shall be indicated on the report.
2. For stale checks which have been unclaimed and thus, the
original DV and supporting documents are still with the
Treasurer, the cancelled check shall be presented in the
RCI after the last check issued for the period indicated in
the report. The original DV and supporting documents shall
be returned to the Accountant who shall prepare a JEV to
record the transaction as Accounts Payable.
3. For checks which became spoiled or stale in the hands of
the payee and which require replacement, a new check
may be issued upon submission of the spoiled or stale
check to the Treasurer. A certified copy of the DV shall be
requested from the Auditor for presentation to the
Administrator/Local Chief Executive who shall countersign
the check. The cancelled check shall be reported and
attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation.
The replacement check shall also be reported
chronologically in the RCI.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor send the BAC Chairperson, Members and Secretariat including the members of the Technical Working Group  to training/s on procurement to professionalize  its procurement officials in order to promote and attain good governance by adhering to the principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in its procurement process.
observations.managementAction: A memorandum was issued to BAC Chairman and Members reiterating the order for the BAC Secretariat to prepare an Annual Procurement Plan using the prescribed form based on all PPMPs included in the budget proposals and supplemental APPs thereafter indicating all the required information therein as enumerated in Section 7.3.4 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned municipal official/staff to ensure that any stages of the procurement process of the LGU are implemented within the  bounds of the law pursuant to the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Issued memorandum to BAC to see to it that all stages of the procurement process  of the LGU are carried out within the bounds of the law pursuant to the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality submit: (i) corrected copies of the  property cards and ledger cards; (ii) supporting  schedules for the PPE balances as per FS; and (iii) a properly accomplished, reconciled and approved RPCPPE.

We reiterated our CY 2013 and prior years’ recommendations  that the Municipal Treasurer  come up with an updated Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayers’ Index Card and furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the corresponding amount of tax due and collectible during the year.

Intensify collection efforts of the above receivable accounts by sending collection/demand letters to the delinquent debtor.  Exert effort to reconcile accounting records with the social welfare officer relative to the SEA receivables and also make representation with the Office of the President regarding the PSF loan released to LICA.   Validation of the existence of the SEA and LICA projects should also be made.

We recommended that the Municipality submit proof that such claims are allowable charges against  the 20% Development Fund under  the guidelines, otherwise, discontinue the  practice and provide  appropriation for these charges under the general fund proper.
We recommended that Management submit the lacking documents to avoid audit disallowance of the subject transactions.

We recommended that Management: (i) submit explanation on the deficiencies noted; and (ii) impose the recovery of appropriate liquidated damages otherwise the official/s responsible will be held liable.
We recommended that Management submit (1) explanation on the subject deficiencies and (2) the lacking documents and disbursement vouchers to avoid audit disallowance.

We recommended that Management: 

•	submit a list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented during the year;

•	observe proper notification to the public pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004 and the amendment under COA Circular No. 2016-003; and
•	inform the Supervising  Auditor (SA) and the Audit Team Leader within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the  program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

We recommended that Management submit an explanation on the deficiencies noted on the grant of cash advances for payroll and activities. Henceforth, ensure that cash advances are supported with complete documentation   pursuant to Section 1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct regular physical inventory taking on all properties to validate all recorded balances for PPE items. To facilitate said inventory count, each department may conduct physical inventory on all properties situated within its office, to be submitted for consolidation with those prepared by the others. 2. List all Property, Plant and Equipment found on hand, classifying them into serviceable or unserviceable; 3. Maintain updated property cards per category on all property plant and equipment as recorded in the list of Property, Plant and Equipment of accounting records. 4. Reconcile property records ensuring that the balances shown in the prepared RCPPE agree with the supply records and the book balances in the accounting records, and vice versa; 5. Renew and maintain all acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE); 6. Prepare the necessary Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and Waste Materials Report for the unserviceable properties found on hand, taking into consideration EO No. 888 March 1983, COA Memorandum No. 2005-027. 7. Direct the Municipal Assessor to produce a listing of all the properties owned by the government and reconcile them with those properties counted by the committee; 8. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to determine the validity of the PPE account through reconciliation of the accounting records and the actual inventory; 9. Require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Prepare the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) for each class of property, listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information about the property, plant and equipment, based on the source documents of the transactions; b. Drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties after the Waste Material Report and the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property are duly approved and evaluated by the COA.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive: a. Require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Office as well as concerned LGU officials and personnel to include and properly identify the four (4) priority thematic areas in the preparation of the LDRRMP in consonance with Section 21 of RA No. 10121. c. Require the MDRRMO who is responsible for setting the direction and implementation of DRRM programs to maximize the utilization of the MDRRMF in which programs of the fund will ensure readiness of the LGU in case of calamity. Also, the LDRRMP should properly identify the four (4) priority areas in the preparation. d. Revert to the General Fund the CY 2012 balance in the amount of P1,106,583.70 for GF social services since it was not utilized after five years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Cause the discontinuance of the use of open dumpsites and identification of possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes. The landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with guidelines/criteria mentioned under RA 9003. b. Cause the strict implementation of the policy on the use of personal protective equipment by the garbage collectors and in-charge of the dumpsite. c. Cause the conduct of information campaign to instill awareness on no littering and no open burning policy and the conduct of information/education/communication activities pertaining to solid waste management among the constituents through several strategies. One strategy is including the issues on solid waste management during pre-marriage counseling conducted by the LGU. Another strategy is including the subject during seminars conducted by the Department of Health, Department of Agriculture and other government line agencies. Another strategy is calling barangay representatives to be lectured on the issues concerning solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: Some actions taken including planning for sanitary landfill, PPE implementation; further work needed.

projects and fast-track the implementation of said programs, projects and
activities funded by the BUB, ADM, ECCD and PCF to provide

8.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance
Committee to judiciously determine the projected income to be collected

to effect the necessary Adjusting Journal Entries (AJEs) regarding the

Management officer to revisit the guidelines set forth in CSC
Memorandum Circular No. 24, s. 2002 and CSC MC 26, s. 1997. Further,
we also recommend that vacant positions be filled up to handle works that
require regular employees.

11.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Officer to
consider the appropriate means of communication depending on the
information that it wishes to pass on to the public and its stakeholders. For
instance, there should be public notices on television and on the radio
about who can access the landfill, the working hours at the landfill to limit
after-hours access and the schedule for collection of wastes from
households for the different zones. Websites have become increasingly
effective and economical in disseminating information. Therefore, it is
important to have a specific link for Solid Waste Management within the
Municipality’s website so that it is easier for the public to have access to
information and policy on solid waste management as well as any issue
concerning solid waste management.

11.9 To ensure effective implementation of the solid waste management plan, a
well-designed, current and targeted community awareness program is
required. The targeted audience should be considered when determining
how the information should be given to the public. The urgency of the
matter should also be considered so that everyone is aware of the matter as
soon as possible.

12.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Solid
Management Program Focal Person to maintain disposal site records in the
Residual Containment Area (RCA).

13.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor caused the fencing of the
RCA with a secured gate at the entrance to minimize illegal visitors or
scavengers on the site. This will also help address the occupational health
and safety issues by limiting access.

14.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor caused the installation of a
mechanism to measure the volume of waste and type of waste to be placed
at the site, which will provide baseline data to assist in developing
effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for waste
management.

15.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide technical support and
appropriate funds for the waste segregation at the barangay level; require
the SWM Officer inspect the waste to confirm the type of waste to be
disposed then direct the vehicle to the designated area depending on the
type of waste already inspected.

15.7 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Waste
Management Unit to implement a color coding or appropriate means of
identification to ensure that wastes are properly segregated at the RCA.

16.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person
to train personnel to be competent in handling crisis and conflicts who
will manage the VAWC Help Desk in the barangays.

17.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person
to establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System.

Accountant to exert effort in enforcing the immediate settlement of audit
disallowances and, ensure that all employees who are retiring or
transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and
charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA
Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

Accountant to make reconciliation of their records against that of the
GSIS. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant shall also verify who
among the employees is deficient in paying the loan obligations so that an
adjustment in his/her deduction shall be made.

observations.recommendation:
That the Local Chief Executive, as the Chairman of the LPRAT:
a) Direct the designated BUB Focal Person to coordinate with the different head of offices regarding their program and project with BUB fundings.
b) Direct the Municipal Engineer to fast-tract the implementation of programs and projects funded by the National government in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA.

That the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere to Section 287 of RA 7160 and DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 and ensure that only projects aligned with purpose/objectives of the 20% Development Fund be funded from such appropriation.

a. Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to maximize the utilization of the MDRRMF in order to achieve the objectives of the fund and ensure readiness of the LGU in times of calamity. Also, local officials and the members of the LDRRMC are enjoined to effect the implementation of the programs, projects and activities related to disaster prevention and management.
b. Require the LDRRMO to include and properly identify the four (4) priority thematic areas in the preparation of the LDRRMP in consonance with Section 21 of RA No. 10121.
c. Require the MDRRMO who is responsible for setting the direction and implementation of the programs to maximize the utilization of the MDRRMF in which will ensure readiness of the LGU in times of calamity.
d. Revert to the General Fund the unexpended balance in the Local Disaster Fund from CY 2012 to CY 2016 amounting to P5,530,552.69 since it was not utilized after five years.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive compel the LCPC to fast track the implementation of LCPC programs. Likewise, the LCPC’s Annual Work and Financial Plan, should only include programs, projects and activities which will cater for the benefit and welfare of the children.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local School board to strictly observe the DepEd, DBM, DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 on the utilization of Special Education Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Human Resource Management Officer to review the duties and functions of the JOs so as not to duplicate the duties and functions of the regular plantilla employees, and compel the Municipal Budget Officer to adjust the rates of Job Order Workers in compliance to regulations.

1. Require the Municipal DRRMO to maximize the utilization of the MDRRMF in order to achieve the objectives of the fund and ensure readiness of the LGU in times of calamity.
2. Require the Municipal Treasurer that the amount withheld shall be remitted to GSIS in full within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the withholding was accrued.
3. Require the concerned employee in-charge in the preparation of GSIS remittances and municipal accountant to conduct thorough reconciliation with its records with that of the GSIS.

We also recommended that the Mayor provide the necessary financial and technical support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own Materials Recovery Facility according to standards and strictly implement waste segregation.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to strictly adhere to section 37.4.2 of RA 9184, i.e., to post in the PhilGEPS Notices of Awards, Notice of Proceed, Notice of Award, and Approved Contract of Agreements for transparency and information in all procurement processes.

1. Compel the Municipal Accountant to review the details of the Due from LGUs and Other Receivable accounts and exert all efforts to locate records to prove the validity of claim from the identified recipients of these funds.
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the reportorial requirements on funds transferred to local or national government agencies or and other debtors.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to revert to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund the remaining PDAF funds in accordance with the Supreme Court Decision of November 19, 2013.

We recommend the LGU to take final action on the auction sale with proper mode of its disposal. Prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and Waste Materials Report and take the appropriate action for disposal.

For the fast-tracking of the implementation of the 20% Development projects to provide constituents of the benefits due them.

We enjoined the local officials for the utilization of the unexpended balance for disaster risk reduction and management program, project & activities and include the activities to be funded from the unexpended balance in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the strict adherence to Section 287 of RA 7160 and ensure that only projects aligned with the purpose/objectives of the 20% Development Fund be funded from such appropriation.

We recommended that the BAC stop the practice of delaying the procurement projects and adhere strictly to Annex C of the RIRR which sets the minimum and maximum allowable time to act on various activities from posting/advertisement for issuance of NTP.

We recommended that the LCE direct all concerned employees to: a) Make sure that all NOA and NTP are posted in the PhilGEPS; b) Follow the provisions of the Contract Agreement between the LGU and the contractor which require the latter the submission of project documents that are necessary for the project implementation.

We recommended for the adoption of a decentralized solid waste management system by providing financial and technical assistance for the establishment/operation of functional and standard-based MRFs in the barangays and for a wide dissemination of information on source reduction, recycling and composting programs in conformance to the IRR of RA 9003.

a. Cause the discontinuance of the use of open dumpsites and identification of possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes. This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned under RA 9003.
b. Cause the strict implementation of the policy on the use of personal protective equipment by the garbage collectors and in-charge of the dumpsite.
c. Cause the conduct of information campaign to instill awareness on no littering and no open burning policy and the conduct of information/education/communication activities pertaining to solid waste management among the constituents through several strategies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere to the rules concerning the use of the 20% Development Fund. In addition, programs, projects and activities shall be formulated in accordance with the guidelines set forth under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-001 dated April 13, 2011.

a) Require the employees concerned to adhere strictly to Section 89 of P.D. 1445; Sections 5.7 and 8 of COA Circular 97-002 and COA Circular 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and enforce sanctions in case of failure of the accountable officer to liquidate his/her cash advances on time.
b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to accountable officers who were granted cash advance for its liquidation thereof.

That the Local Chief Executive direct the personnel in-charge in the preparation of Memorandum of Agreement to include a provision that the implemented project must be validated upon completion through conduct of inspection by the Municipal Engineer and prepare inspection report.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts to intensify tax collection. Tax information campaign and rewards should be vigorously explored and pursued to encourage payment. Publish delinquent properties and send billing notices to defaulting taxpayers. Request Provincial Assessor to enforce possible remedies such as auction, levy and distraint of personal property to recover the tax delinquency.

c. The infrastructure contracts be reviewed and revised to include a provision for arbitration process so that subsequent contracts which will be entered into by the LGU would comply with the relevant requirements of RA 9184 IRR and related laws.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1) Direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to conduct regular physical inventory
taking on all properties to validate all recorded balances for PPE items. To
facilitate said inventory count, each department may conduct physical count of
properties situated within its office, to be submitted for consolidation with those
prepared by the others:
a) List all Property, Plant and Equipment found on hand, classifying them
into serviceable or unserviceable;
b) Maintain updated property cards per category on all property plant and
equipment as recorded in the list of Property, Plant and Equipment of
accounting records.
c) Reconcile property records ensuring that the balances shown in the
prepared RCPPE agree with the supply records and the book balances in
the accounting records, and vice versa;
d) Renew and maintain all acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE)
for the signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared RCPPE;
e) Prepare the necessary Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property and Waste Materials Report for the unserviceable properties
found on hand, taking into consideration EO No. 888 March 1983, COA
Memorandum No. 2005-027.
2. Direct the Municipal Assessor to produce a listing of all the properties owned by
the government and reconcile them with those properties counted by the
committee with an explanation for those items that are recorded in the books but
could not be physically located;
3. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to determine the
validity of the PPE account through reconciliation of the accounting records and
the actual inventory;
4. Require the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Prepare the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) for
each class of property, plant and equipment listing therein the data as to
its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation,
disposal, and other information about the property, plant and equipment,
based on the source documents of the transactions;
b) Drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties after the
Waste Material Report and the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property are duly approved and evaluated by the COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the officials concerned be directed to submit the required documents otherwise, all those who authorized the payment of the subject allowance shall be held personally liable for the amount involved.  Likewise, the elective officials concerned are required to refund the amount received.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013No.10

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to comply with the procurement procedures prescribed under R.A. 9184 and its RIRR and to exercise due diligence in the processing of claims. We further recommended that before effecting payment complete documentation must be observed.
observations.reason: Some bidding documents attached to disbursement vouchers. lacked required documentations, thus, not in conformity with the procurement procedures prescribed under R.A. 9184 and its RIRR and to exercise due diligence in the processing of claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the fast-tracking of implementation of development projects to provide constituents of the benefits due them.
observations.reason: Some programmed projects still not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer to: a) Strictly comply with Annex A and Annex E of the IRR of RA 9184 and assure that in no case shall a variation order exceed 10% of the original contract cost. b) Make sure that any increase or decrease of work items, including the introduction of new work items that are not included in the original contract, must be supported with a change order with corresponding revised plans or designs, if applicable. c) Strictly comply with the Manual of Procedures for the Procurement of Infrastructure Projects and make sure that any deletion of work does not result in cumulative negative variation order. d) Coordinate with the DPWH-SLDEO prior to the actual procurement activities to avoid duplication of projects, thus, preventing wastage of fund as well as delay in project implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate revert back the outstanding checks ranging from two (2) years to sixteen (16) years in the books as debit to cash in bank and credit prior year’s adjustment duly supported with necessary required documents.
observations.reason: Outstanding checks ranging from two (2) years to sixteen (16) years were treated in the books; action taken to revert them as prior year adjustments as recommended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure strict adherence to Section 287 of RA 7160 and Memorandum Circular No. 2010-138 dated December 2, 2010, and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and to ensure that only projects aligned with purpose/objectives of the 20% Development Fund be funded from such appropriation.
observations.reason: Expenses for 1st quarter of the year as well as programmed projects still charged to 20% DF as counterpart.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive remind the school administrators in their respective locality to revise their School Improvement Plan to specifically include the expenses enumerated under DepEd Order No. 12, s. 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive remind the Municipal School Board to limit the utilization of the SEF to the programs and projects defined under Section 272 of RA 7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998.
We recommended that the LCE direct the MBO to ensure that PAPs for PRDP and BUB Projects charged against 20% DF are utilized only for capital expenditures or in the nature of investment.
observations.reason: There were programmed expenses charged not aligned with the purpose of 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials to submit to the Office of the Auditor all suspension orders or any documents that would warrant the non-imposition of liquidated damages to the concerned contractors/suppliers, otherwise, the Municipal Accountant shall compute the liquidated damages based on the provisions of Sections 8.1 and 8.3 of the RIRR of RA 9184. We also recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to prepare a report on the unperformed portions of the aforementioned infrastructure projects based on their respective expected completion date.
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer to conduct inspection of projects immediately after the reported date of completion to determine whether projects were completed on time.
observations.reason: There were delays in inspections and some extensions approved without required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC shall adhere strictly to Section 12.1 (13) b of RIRR of RA 9184 which states that only the BAC with the assistance of TWG shall undertake the post-proceedings.
observations.reason: Post-qualification proceedings were sometimes conducted without the required composition of BAC and TWG.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC stop the practice of delaying the procurement process and adhere strictly to Annex C of the RIRR which sets the minimum and maximum allowable time to act on various procurement activities from posting/advertisement for issuance of NTP.
observations.reason: There were delays in procurement actions in some projects resulting in late issuance of Notices to Proceed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman/Secretariat to furnish the Audit Team with copies of the perfected contracts/purchase agreements together with complete supporting documents within five (5) days in compliance with COA Circulars.
observations.reason: There is a delay in the posting of Notice to Proceed and Notice of Award due to internet signal problem; some documents not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 and make sure that all tarpaulin signboards contain details on project status and regular updating on the progress of accomplishments of the infrastructure project be reported thereon.

We recommended for the adoption of a decentralized solid waste management system by providing financial and technical assistance for the establishment/operation of MRF in all barangays and wide dissemination of information on source reduction, recycling and composting program in conformance to the provisions of the IRR of RA 9003.
observations.reason: MRFs were constructed in many barangays but some MRFs are not in accordance with the standard.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Cause the discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and identification of possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes in accordance with RA 9003; b) Cause the strict implementation of the policy on the use of personal protective equipment by the garbage collectors and in-charge of the dumpsite; c) Cause the conduct of information campaign to instill awareness on no littering and no open burning policy and the conduct of information/education/communication activities pertaining to solid waste management among the constituents through several strategies.
observations.reason: Safe Closure and rehabilitation plan is yet to be undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere to the rules concerning the use of the 20% Economic Development Fund for the attainment of the purposes of said fund and that programs, projects and activities shall be formulated in accordance with guidelines set forth under DILG-DBM.
observations.reason: There were expenses incurred not aligned with the circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Require the employees concerned to adhere strictly to Section 89 of P.D. 1445; COA Circular 97-002; COA Circular 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and enforce sanctions in case of failure of the accountable officer to liquidate on time. b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to accountable officers who were granted cash advances for its liquidation.
observations.reason: Some employees settled their cash advances but others failed to liquidate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the personnel in-charge to prepare Memorandum of Agreement to include a provision that the implemented project must be validated upon completion through conduct of inspection by the Municipal Engineer and prepare inspection report.
observations.reason: Demand letters were sent; some reconciliation and inspections pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts to intensify tax collection. Tax information campaign and rewards should be explored to encourage payment. Also direct the Municipal Assessor to send letter request to the Provincial Assessor to enforce possible remedies such as auction, levy and distraint of personal property to recover tax delinquency.
observations.reason: Target set by the BLGF still too high and revisions on assessed value required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive remind the Municipal School Board to limit the utilization of the SEF to programs and projects defined under Section 272 of RA 7160 and DepEd/DBM/DILG Circular No. 01.

We recommended that whenever medicines are procured the purchase request should bear a certification from the requisitioning officer that the procured items are in compliance with established regulations; require that the Property Custodian submit Accounting Monthly Report of Medicines Issued as a basis for dropping from inventory account and taking up Medicines Expenses.
observations.reason: The RHU failed to submit inventory reports; some corrective actions taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Vice-Mayor and SB Members exercise prudence in spending and send participants only to seminars/trainings who are directly in charge for the agenda of the conference called for and that seminars/trainings for participants from the province be conducted within the province to minimize cost.
observations.reason: Some trainings/seminars still attended outside the region by SB Members; measures recommended to avoid excess costs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to comply with all the requirements of RA 9184, including preparation of detailed engineering surveys and designs and attach certification of adequacy to the contract documents; that infrastructure contracts include provisions for arbitration; and ensure subsequent contracts comply with RA 9184 IRR.
observations.reason: There is delay in preparation of detailed engineering surveys and designs for some projects; some corrective steps initiated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant to submit the accounts of the municipality regularly on every 10th day of the succeeding month and that accountable officers turn-over source documents within five (5) days after the end of each month.
observations.reason: Some vouchers for unclaimed checks were still pending; improvements in submission being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prepare the necessary documents for the titling of these parcels of land and allocate in the annual budget funds needed to secure Transfer Certificate of Title for the LGU’s real estate.
observations.reason: DENR has a calendar of activities to be followed; the LGU to wait for schedule of free titling activities acquired.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit the required report/s pursuant to  RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-02 otherwise, the allowable sanctions authorized by law shall be implemented.

We recommend that the municipal officials concerned submit to COA within seven (7) calendar days after release of the check pursuant to the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit promptly to the auditor the financial statements within the prescribed period as provided for under Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 and Section 41 (2) of  PD 1445 otherwise, the allowed sanctions will be implemented pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to  trace the reasons for the difference of the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities per fund so that the corresponding adjustments could be made to ensure the reliability and accuracy of its presentation in the Cash Flows Statement.
