We recommended for the adoption of a decentralized solid waste
management system by providing financial and technical assistance for the
establishment /operation of functional and standard-based MRFs in the
barangays of the Municipality. We likewise recommended for a wide
dissemination of information on source reduction, recycling and
composting program in conformance to the provision of the IRR of RA 9003.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Cause the discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and
identification of possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final
disposal site for solid wastes. This landfill shall be designed and operated in
accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned under RA 9003.
b. Cause the strict implementation of the policy on the use of personal
protective equipment by the garbage collectors and in-charge of the
dumpsite.
c. Cause the conduct of information campaign to instill awareness on no
littering and no open burning policy and the conduct of
information/education/communication activities pertaining to solid
waste management among the constituents through several strategies. One
strategy is including the issues on solid waste management during pre-
marriage counseling conducted by the LGU. Another strategy is including
the subject during seminars conducted by the Department of Health,
Department of Agriculture and other government line agencies. Another
strategy is calling barangay representatives to be lectured on the issues
concerning solid waste management.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Property Officer in-charge to submit the Report of Property Plant and Equipment as of December 31, 2015.
observations.managementAction: AAR Inventory Finding Report as of December 31, 2016 was submitted to the auditor on January 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to check all claims as to completeness of documentations before they are paid. Such measure must be carried out to preclude the suspension or disallowance of transactions during post-audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding were submitted to COA for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to promptly and regularly prepare the required Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF which shall be subject to audit by COA and to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF-MOOE to the prescribed special trust fund account to protect the fund against misuse.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly FUR for LDRRMF Finding were submitted to COA and unexpended balance were transferred to the special trust fund account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere to the rules concerning the use of the 20% Economic Development Fund for the attainment of the purposes of said fund. In addition, programs, projects and activities shall be formulated in accordance with the guidelines set forth under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-001 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The LCE made sure that only those of current transactions authorized by the provided laws were payments charged to 20% DF
observations.reason: Per verification those payments were not authorized by the said Joint Memorandum Circular hence reiterated in this year’s audit observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Require the employees concerned to adhere strictly to Section 89 of P.D. 1445; COA Circular 97-002 specifically Sections 5.7 and 8 and the COA Circular 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and enforce sanctions in case of failure of the accountable officer to liquidate his/her cash advances on time. b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to accountable officers who were granted cash advance for its liquidation thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was instructed to adhere strictly to Section 89 of P.D. 1445; COA Circular 97-002 and COA Circular 2012-004 and the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to accountable officers for liquidation.
observations.reason: There were long outstanding cash advances for those AO who were currently no longer in government service or died already.

observations.recommendation: We recomended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all her collection including all collections turned over to her daily or not later than the next banking day. Initiate disciplinary measure against collecting officers who are perennially delayed in remitting their collections to avoid the risk of misuse or loss of funds.
observations.managementAction: Undeposited cash collections were deposited intact on the first banking day of the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the personnel in-charge in the preparation of Memorandum of Agreement to include a financial provision that the implemented project must be validated upon completion through the conduct of inspection by the Municipal Engineer and prepare inspection report.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant instructed personnel in-charge to include a financial provision that the implemented project must be validated upon completion through the conduct of inspection by the Municipal Engineer and prepare inspection report.
observations.reason: Monitoring team failed to conduct inspection because the implementing barangays were granted engineering assistance. The engineering office was not informed by barangays about the status of implementation of their project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts to intensify tax collection. Tax information campaign and rewards should be vigorously explored and pursued to encourage payment. It would be wiser also to cause the publication of delinquent properties and send billing notices to defaulting taxpayers. We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Assessor to send letter request to the Provincial Assessor to enforce possible remedies.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had been sending billing notices to defaulting taxpayers.
observations.reason: Still it was difficult to meet the target set by BLGF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the adherence to the provisions of Sec. 10, Article IV of RA 9184, which requires that all procurement shall be done through competitive public bidding through the Bids & Awards Committee to ensure that procurement of goods and services are obtained at prices which are most advantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 9 instructed to minimize payment other than those pertains to travelling expenses.
observations.reason: Payment other than those pertains to travelling expenses were minimized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive remind the Municipal School Board to limit the utilization of the SEF to the programs and projects defined under RA 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated April 14, 1998 in order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail under this fund.
observations.managementAction: After the exit conference, the LGU made sure that utilization of the funds is in accordance with rules.

observations.recommendation: Whenever medicines are procured the purchase request should bear a certification from the requisitioning officer that the procured items are in compliant with PNDF vol. I current edition. Also, we require that the Property Custodian submit to Accounting Monthly Report of Medicines Issued as a basis for dropping from inventory account and taking up Medicines Expenses.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had attached the required documents to the DVs. The required documents to the DVs were attached.
observations.reason: Small Value Procurement documents for medicines were not required; some required documents were not attached to the DVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Vice-Mayor and Municipal Mayor instruct participants to exercise prudence in spending government funds, specifically: a. Seminars/trainings where participants come from the province should be conducted within the province in order to minimize the cost of the conduct of these activities and save funds for other relative projects. b. Send participants only to seminars who are directly in charge for the agenda of the conference called for. c. Make representation with the Office of the PCL to conform with the regulations on seminars/trainings as mandated by COA and DILG.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed to minimize or refrain in trainings/trainings. PCL Southern Leyte with venue in Cebu City.
observations.reason: SB members still attend trainings/seminars with venue in Cebu City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to comply with all the requirement of RA 9184, its implementing rules and all laws, decrees, circulars, memoranda and issuances governing infrastructure projects of local governments. Specifically, we recommend that: a. All procurement should be meticulously and judiciously planned by the procuring entity. b. A certification by the Municipal Engineer that the detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted according to the prescribed agency standards and specifications in conformance with the provisions of Annex A should be attached in the contract and that the detailed engineering outputs are adequate for the procurement at hand. c. The infrastructure contracts be reviewed and revised to include a provision for arbitration process so that subsequent contracts which will be entered into by the LGU would comply with the relevant requirements of RA 9184 IRR and related laws.
observations.managementAction: The required documents are on file with the Municipal Engineer. The required documents are attached to the DVs.
observations.reason: The required documents were attached to the DVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting full Transportation Allowances to LGU officials who are issued or actually using government vehicle. Full adherence to Section 56 of the General Appropriations Act of 2015, Section VI of COA Circular No. 75-6 and Local Budget Circular No. 103 is required. Utilization of government vehicle should be closely monitored by the office assigned to prepare the payroll for the Representation and Transportation Allowances. Proportionate amount pertaining to the actual days when government transportation facilities were used should be deducted from their monthly Transportation Allowances. We also recommended that each trip ticket be properly accomplished and the authorized passenger officials be indicated.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The management had not acted on this recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts of the municipality regularly on every 10th day of the succeeding month. Likewise, all accountable officers are to turn-over to the accounting division for recording purposes as well as source documents within five (5) days after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: Personnel in-charge in the preparation of vouchers and cause dissemination of information to claimants right after the issuance of reports checks and give cut off period of the release.
observations.reason: Always the cause of delayed checks is submission of vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman/Secretariat furnish the Audit Team with copies of the perfected contracts together with the complete supporting documents within five (5) days after issuance in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Personnel assigned in the preparation of vouchers and those handling submission to COA are instructed to submit perfected contracts together with the complete supporting documents within five (5) days after issuance.
observations.reason: In practice, contracts are attached to the vouchers and submitted to COA only when the vouchers are submitted.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the Municipal Assessor to prepare the necessary documents for the titling of these parcels of land to safeguard municipality’s ownership of land/lots acquired. b. We further recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan and the Budget officer to allocate in the annual budget funds needed to secure Transfer Certificate of Title for the LGU’s real estate.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 1 instruct the Sangguniang Bayan and Budget Officer to allocate funds needed to secure Transfer Certificate of Title for the LGU’s real estate.
observations.reason: Some parcels of land titling is on process.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the contractor/supplier to submit the corresponding Certificates of Product Registration. b. The Technical Working Group (TWG) of the Bids and Awards Committee should also verify the authenticity of the CPRs submitted. c. Require the supplier to submit a Certificate of Good Manufacturing Practice and Certificate of Authority as a Distributor from the manufacturer.
observations.managementAction: The required documents are on file. The TWG is instructed to verify the authenticity of the CPRs submitted.
observations.reason: Purchases of medicines still lack the required documents.
